name: Caro-Kann Defense
description: A solid, resilient defense where Black prepares to challenge the center with ...d5 from a stable base. Less tactical than the Sicilian but more dynamic than the French — the Caro-Kann rewards patience and good endgame technique.

moves.instruction: Claim the center and open lines for the bishop and queen.
The Caro-Kann. Prepare ...d5 with pawn support from c6.
Build a broad pawn center, accepting the challenge.
Strike at the center. The defining move of the Caro-Kann.
Defend e4 and develop a piece toward the center.
Trade in the center, opening the c8-bishop's diagonal.
Recapture with the knight — the Classical variation.
Develop the bishop before castling, while the e6-square is still free.
Attack the bishop and weaken its grip, gaining a tempo.
Retreat the bishop to a safe diagonal that still eyes the queenside.
Gain space on the kingside and threaten h5 to undermine Black's structure.
Secure the g5-square and prevent any knight jumps into that square.
Develop the kingside knight and prepare to castle.
Avoid the ...Ngf6 jump (which walks into checks) and keep flexibility.
Place the bishop on a strong diagonal, eyeing f7 and supporting attack.
Solidify the center and open another path for the f8-bishop.
